rationale: The course materials explicitly identify Artificial Intelligence (AI), Robotics, and Quantum Computing as the emerging technologies reshaping computing and fundamentally altering how societies function.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: Why are emerging technologies described as fundamentally altering how societies function rather than merely improving existing technologies?
option_a: They only make minor improvements to old systems
option_b: They create entirely new capabilities — such as autonomous decision-making, quantum-level computation, and human-robot collaboration — that change economic structures, workforce requirements, and social interactions
option_c: They have no impact on society
option_d: They are too expensive to affect society
rationale: The course materials describe these technologies as fundamentally altering societies because they enable capabilities that did not previously exist, transforming economies, improving quality of life, and solving complex problems in ways that incremental improvements cannot.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: A city implements AI-powered traffic management that reduces congestion by 30% but critics note it could be used for mass surveillance of citizens' movements. Which governance approach best addresses both the benefits and concerns?
option_a: Deploy the system without any oversight
option_b: Ban the technology entirely
option_c: Implement transparent governance frameworks that maximise quality-of-life improvements while establishing clear privacy protections and oversight mechanisms
option_d: Ignore the surveillance concerns
rationale: The course materials note that emerging technologies introduce significant ethical dilemmas alongside their potential. Transparent governance that preserves benefits while addressing concerns through privacy protections and oversight represents a balanced approach to managing these dual aspects.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: Which analysis best explains why robotics is classified as an emerging technology distinct from traditional industrial automation?
option_a: Robotics and traditional automation are identical
option_b: Traditional automation is more advanced than robotics
option_c: Robotics does not involve any mechanical components
option_d: Modern robotics incorporates AI-driven adaptation, learning, and human collaboration capabilities that go beyond the repetitive pre-programmed movements of traditional industrial automation
rationale: While traditional automation follows pre-programmed instructions, modern Robotics as an emerging technology incorporates adaptive capabilities, sensor integration, and AI that enable dynamic interaction with unpredictable environments and human collaborators.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: An agricultural cooperative uses AI to predict crop yields, drones to monitor field conditions, and quantum computing models to optimise supply chain logistics. Which statement best describes this technological approach?
option_a: Multiple emerging technologies applied together to transform economic productivity and solve complex problems across different domains of the operation
option_b: Only one emerging technology is being used
option_c: These are not emerging technologies
option_d: Emerging technologies cannot be combined
rationale: The scenario demonstrates AI (predictive analytics), Robotics (drones), and Quantum Computing (supply chain optimisation) — all three emerging technologies from the course materials — applied synergistically to transform agricultural productivity.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: A developer claims that because quantum computers can solve problems exponentially faster, they will replace all conventional computers within five years. Which evaluation best assesses this prediction?
option_a: This is certain because quantum computers are superior for all tasks
option_b: This is overstated because quantum computing currently excels at specific problem types but is not universally superior; conventional computers remain more practical for everyday tasks, and widespread replacement depends on overcoming significant technical challenges
option_c: Quantum computing is not a real technology
option_d: Conventional computers will never be replaced for any purpose
rationale: While Quantum Computing offers exponential speedup for specific problem classes, it is not universally superior to conventional computing. The course materials present it as one of three emerging technologies with transformative potential, not as an immediate replacement for all existing systems.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: Which analysis best explains the connection between emerging technologies and the workforce planning challenges discussed in earlier course modules?
option_a: Emerging technologies have no impact on workforce needs
option_b: Workforce planning becomes unnecessary with advanced technology
option_c: Emerging technologies like AI and robotics create new specialisations while displacing traditional roles, requiring workforce planning to forecast demands and develop or acquire new competencies
option_d: Emerging technologies eliminate all jobs permanently
rationale: The course materials link emerging technologies to both transformative potential and ethical dilemmas including workforce transformation. This connects directly to the workforce planning module's focus on forecasting labour demands and managing skill gaps in a fast-changing technological environment.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: A developing nation is considering investing in quantum computing research infrastructure. Which factor should most influence this decision based on course concepts?
option_a: Only the potential to transform economies
option_b: Only the ethical dilemmas involved
option_c: The nation's current digital infrastructure and skills gap, because emerging technologies require foundational capabilities; without addressing the digital divide and skills gap first, the benefits may not be widely accessible
option_d: Only what other nations are doing
rationale: The course materials identify both the potential of emerging technologies and the reality of the digital divide and skills gaps. For developing nations, foundational digital infrastructure and human capability must be in place for emerging technology investments to deliver equitable benefits.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: Some argue that the ethical dilemmas of emerging technologies are sufficient reason to halt their development until perfect governance frameworks exist. Which evaluation best assesses this position?
option_a: This is impractical because the course materials suggest these technologies carry immense potential that could be lost with indefinite delay; governance should evolve alongside technology rather than attempting to be perfect before any development occurs
option_b: This is the only responsible approach
option_c: Emerging technologies have no potential benefits worth pursuing
option_d: Perfect governance frameworks already exist
rationale: The course materials present emerging technologies as having both immense potential and significant ethical dilemmas. An indefinite moratorium would forego transformative benefits, whereas iterative governance development can address ethical concerns while preserving innovation capacity.

course_name: SSC 202 — Introduction to Computer and Its Application
module_name: Topic 9: Emerging Technologies and Future of Computing
stem: Which analysis best synthesises how the three emerging technologies (AI, Robotics, Quantum Computing) collectively represent a new stage in the industrial revolution framework from Week 1?
option_a: They represent no change from previous industrial stages
option_b: They represent the technological realisation of the Global Tech Era — the fourth stage that brings about AI — fulfilling the course's historical framework by creating the intelligent, automated, and computationally advanced systems that define this era
option_c: They are unrelated to industrial development
option_d: They represent a return to the Agrarian Period
rationale: The Four Stages of Industrial Revolution culminate in the Global Tech Era, defined as bringing about Artificial Intelligence. The three emerging technologies — AI, Robotics, and Quantum Computing — represent the technological manifestation of this fourth stage, connecting the historical framework to contemporary technological reality.
